originalTitle: Zimbabwe says 15 nationals killed after being lured to fight for Russia
title: Zimbabwe Helps Its People Come Home
content:
Our Friends from Zimbabwe

Hello, my little news friends! Today we have an important story from a country called Zimbabwe. It is a beautiful country in Africa, just like ours! In Zimbabwe, people are kind and caring. They love their families and their friends. A long time ago, some people from Zimbabwe went on a very long trip. They left their homes and their families. They went to a faraway land, hoping to find new jobs or to learn new things. They thought they were going to a big country called Russia.

Going on a Big Adventure?

Imagine going on a trip across the world! That's what some grown-ups from Zimbabwe did. They were told there were good opportunities for them in Russia. Maybe a new job, or a chance to study. It sounded like an exciting adventure, a chance to help their families back home. **Did you know?** Russia is the biggest country in the world! It is so big, it crosses two continents, Europe and Asia. It is also very, very far from Africa. Flying there takes many, many hours!

A Tricky Situation

But sometimes, things are not what they seem. When these people arrived in Russia, the adventure turned tricky. It was not the kind of job or school they were promised. They found themselves in a difficult and dangerous place. They were far from home and needed help. Some people even got very, very sick or hurt. Others, sadly, lost their lives. This made everyone in Zimbabwe very sad. It’s like when you go to play, but then something bad happens, and you need your grown-ups to help you. **Did you know?** It’s always important to talk to your parents or a trusted grown-up before you go anywhere far away or meet new people, especially if someone offers you something that seems too good to be true!

Zimbabwe to the Rescue!

When the grown-ups in Zimbabwe heard about their people in trouble, they knew they had to help. The government of Zimbabwe is like a big parent for all the people in the country. Their job is to keep everyone safe and help them when they need it most. So, the government started working very hard. They wanted to find out what happened to everyone. They wanted to make sure their friends and family members were safe. They wanted to bring them back home to Zimbabwe. It’s a very important job, helping their own people. **Did you know?** A country's government is like the head of a very big family. They make rules to keep everyone safe, build schools, and even help people who are in other countries!

Coming Back Home

The government of Zimbabwe is talking to the government of Russia. They are asking for help to bring everyone home. For those who are still alive, they want to make sure they can come back safely to their families. Imagine how happy their families will be to see them again! And for those who sadly lost their lives, the government wants to bring their bodies back home too. This is called "repatriating bodies." It means helping their families say goodbye in their own country. It's a way of showing respect and remembering them. It’s important to remember our friends and family, even when they are gone. **Did you know?** When someone travels to another country, their own country usually gives them a special book called a passport. This passport shows who they are and helps their country keep track of them and help them if they get into trouble.

Grown-Ups Talking Together

How do countries talk to each other? Well, they have special people called diplomats. These are like friendly messengers. The grown-ups from Zimbabwe are talking to the grown-ups from Russia. They are having important conversations. They are trying to understand everything that happened. They want to make sure no more people get into this kind of trouble. It's like when you and your friend have a disagreement, and a grown-up helps you talk it out. Countries do the same thing to solve big problems. **Did you know?** When countries talk to each other to solve problems, it's called "diplomacy." It helps everyone live peacefully together, even if they are far apart!

Always Stay Safe!

This story reminds us of a very important lesson. Always be careful when someone offers you something that seems too good to be true. Always talk to your parents, guardians, or a trusted teacher before making big plans, especially about traveling far away or meeting new people. It’s always best to stay with people you know and trust. Your family and grown-ups love you and want to keep you safe. They will always tell you the truth and protect you. Let's remember to be kind and look out for each other, just like Zimbabwe is looking out for its people. We hope that all the people from Zimbabwe who are still in Russia will come home safe and sound very soon. And we send our love to the families who have lost someone. We are all one big African family, and we care for each other.
summary: Zimbabwe is working with Russia to bring home its people who traveled there and got into trouble, making sure those still alive return safely and those who lost their lives are brought back to their families.
whyMatters: It is important for countries to protect their people and help them, especially when they are far from home and need help, to keep everyone safe and show that every life matters.

originalTitle: Video: GCC chief says Iran charging for ships to pass Strait of Hormuz
title: Ships, Seas, and a Special Water Road!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello there, amazing explorers! It's your friend, Aisha, your kid-friendly news reporter, right here in Africa! I've got a super interesting story for you today. It's about big, big ships, far-away seas, and a special water road that many countries use. So, grab your imaginary binoculars and let's go on an adventure! Today, we're talking about something important happening far, far away, across the big blue ocean. It's about a very narrow path of water that big ships love to use. But someone is saying that one country might be asking for money for ships to pass through!

What is this Special Water Road?

Imagine a super important shortcut. Not for cars, but for giant ships! This shortcut is a narrow path of water. It is called the Strait of Hormuz. Say it with me: Hor-muz. It's like a very busy road, but it's made of sparkling blue water! This Strait of Hormuz connects two very big water bodies. One is called the Persian Gulf, and the other is the Arabian Sea, which then leads to the Indian Ocean. Think of it as a gate between two huge swimming pools! Many, many ships use this water road every single day. It's super important for countries all over the world. Did you know? A 'strait' is a narrow passage of water. It's like a watery hallway that connects two bigger rooms of water! It helps ships travel faster and easier.

Who are the Grown-Ups Talking?

So, who is talking about this? Well, there are two main groups. One is like a special club of countries. This club is called the GCC. GCC stands for Gulf Cooperation Council. It's a group of countries that are friends and work together. They want to help each other and keep things fair and safe. The head of this GCC club, a man named Mr. Jasem al-Budaiwi, has said something important. He said that another country, called Iran, is asking for money. Iran is a big country in Asia, not too far from these water roads. Did you know? Countries often form 'clubs' or 'councils' to talk about problems and find good solutions together! It's like a team trying to solve a puzzle.

What's the Problem?

Here is the puzzle! Mr. Jasem says that Iran is asking for money from ships. They want a special payment for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. This payment is called a 'fee' or a 'charge'. It's like paying a small amount to use a special road, but this is a water road! Imagine you want to use a special swing in the park. And someone says, "Pay me money to use this swing!" That's a bit like what is happening. The GCC club says this is not right. They believe that ships should be able to pass through this water road freely and safely, without having to pay extra money.

Why Do Ships Use This Water Road So Much?

Ah, ships! They are like giant floating trucks! They carry so many important things across the oceans. They carry oil, which helps cars drive, and helps make electricity. They also carry toys, clothes, food, electronics, and so many other things that we use every single day in our homes and schools! This Strait of Hormuz is the quickest and easiest way for many ships to travel between countries that have oil and countries that need oil. It's also vital for carrying other goods to and from faraway places. Without it, ships would have to travel a much, much longer way, taking more time and using more fuel. Did you know? The biggest container ships in the world can be as long as four football fields! They can carry thousands and thousands of big metal boxes, full of things for us!

Why Does This Matter to Us in Africa?

"But Aisha," you might say, "that's so far away! Why should I care?" Well, my clever friends, it matters a lot! Even though it's far, far away from many parts of Africa, the things that happen there can affect us. Think about it: many of the things we use in Africa, like your favourite juice, or a new pair of shoes, or even the petrol for your parent's car, might have traveled on one of these big ships. They come from countries far away, across the seas. If ships have to pay more money to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, then the companies who own the ships will have to pay more. And guess what? Sometimes, they pass that extra cost onto us. This means that the things we buy in our shops might become a little bit more expensive. So, a problem far away can make things cost a bit more right here in our local market! Did you know? Africa trades with many countries around the world! We send out yummy fruits and vegetables, minerals, and other things. And we receive many goods too, like electronics and clothes, all thanks to ships!

What Happens Next?

Right now, all the grown-ups from different countries are talking. They are trying to find a good solution. They want to make sure that ships can travel freely and safely, without anyone asking for unfair money. Everyone wants trade to be smooth and fair for all countries. 'Trade' just means buying and selling things between different countries. It helps countries get what they need and sell what they have. We hope they find a happy answer very soon!

Let's Recap, Super Kids!

So, remember: there's a super important water road called the Strait of Hormuz. Big ships use it to carry precious cargo all over the world. A club of countries, the GCC, says Iran is asking for money from these ships. This could make things more expensive for all of us, even here in Africa. But don't worry! Grown-ups are talking and trying to make sure everything stays fair for everyone. We want ships to sail freely and safely, bringing us all the wonderful things we need and love! That's all for today's news adventure, my little explorers! Stay curious and keep learning! This is Aisha, your news reporter, signing off! See you next time!
summary: The GCC chief says Iran is charging ships to pass through a very important water road called the Strait of Hormuz, which could make things more expensive for everyone, even in Africa.
whyMatters: This matters because if ships have to pay more money to travel through this important water road, it could make the everyday things we buy, like food, toys, and fuel, cost more in our shops in Africa.

originalTitle: 'I have no recollection of anything' - Epstein survivor says she was drugged and raped
title: Brave Voices Make Us Safe!
content:
Hello, My Amazing Friends!

Hello, everyone! It’s your friend from the news, here to talk about something super important. We want all children, like you, to be happy and safe every single day. Being safe means feeling good, not scared, and knowing that grown-ups are there to look after you. Today, we heard a story from a brave grown-up lady. She shared something important with the world.

A Brave Story

Sometimes, when we are little, things can happen that make us feel confused or sad or scared. A long, long time ago, when this lady was younger, she had some unhappy experiences. Someone was not kind to her. This made her feel very bad inside. It was not her fault, and it was not okay. She felt scared to tell anyone for a very long time. Imagine holding a big, heavy secret in your heart! That’s how she felt.

Why We Are Talking About This

Now, she is a grown-up, and she decided to be very, very brave. She decided to share her story with everyone. Why? Not to be sad again, but to help other children and grown-ups. She wants everyone to know that if something bad happens, it’s always okay to talk about it. When we talk, we get help. When we talk, we make things better. Her bravery helps us all learn how important it is to stay safe and speak up.

Your Body, Your Rules!

This is a super important lesson for all of us! Your body belongs to you. No one should ever make you feel uncomfortable with their words or their touch. If someone tries to touch you in a way you don't like, or asks you to do something that feels strange or yucky, you have the power to say “NO!” very loudly. You are the boss of your body! **Did you know?** Your feelings are like a special alarm bell! If something makes you feel yucky or scared, that’s your alarm bell telling you to pay attention and tell a trusted adult!

Who Are Your Super Safe Adults?

Every child needs a team of super safe adults. These are the people you can always talk to, no matter what. Who are they? * Your mom and dad or guardians. * Your grandmas and grandpas. * Your aunties and uncles. * Your teachers at school. * A trusted neighbour or a leader in your community. These grown-ups love you very much, and they want to keep you safe. If something makes you feel bad or scared, run to one of your super safe adults and tell them everything. They will listen, and they will help you. **Did you know?** In many African communities, it’s common for many grown-ups to help look after children! This means you have even more trusted adults around you!

Good Secrets and Bad Secrets

Let’s talk about secrets! Some secrets are fun, like planning a surprise birthday party for your friend. Those are good secrets! They make people happy. But there are also bad secrets. A bad secret is something that someone tells you to keep, but it makes you feel sad, scared, or confused. It might be about something that happened that made you feel yucky. If someone says, "Don't tell anyone," about something that feels bad, that is a bad secret. You must, must, must tell a trusted adult about bad secrets. Telling a bad secret is like letting out a big, heavy sigh, and it makes you feel much lighter and safer.

Being Brave is Being Strong

The grown-up lady who shared her story is incredibly brave. It takes a lot of strength to talk about unhappy things from the past. When she spoke out, she showed everyone that it’s important to be honest and seek help. Her bravery helps to protect other children so they don't have to go through similar experiences. Every time a child or a grown-up speaks up about something that is wrong, they are making our world a safer place for everyone. Your voice is powerful! **Did you know?** Every child in Africa and all over the world has rights! One very important right is to be safe and protected from harm. This means grown-ups have a big job to keep you safe!

Helping Each Other

When we listen to stories like this, it reminds us all to look out for each other. Grown-ups in our homes, schools, and communities need to make sure every child feels safe. If you see another child who looks sad or scared, you can be kind to them. You can tell a trusted adult if you are worried about someone else too. We are all a big family in our African communities, and we must care for each other. **Did you know?** There are special helpers called "child protection officers" or "social workers" in many places. Their job is to help children who might be in danger or who need help finding a safe home.

Our Promise to You

So, my dear friends, remember these important things: 1. Your body is yours. 2. If something feels wrong, say “NO!” 3. Always tell a trusted adult if you are scared or sad. 4. There are no bad secrets when it comes to your safety. 5. Speaking up is brave and strong! We promise to keep bringing you news that helps you learn and stay safe. You are special, you are important, and you deserve to be happy and safe always. Keep smiling, keep playing, and keep talking to your trusted grown-ups!
summary: A brave grown-up lady shared her story about past unhappy experiences to help children understand the importance of speaking up and staying safe with trusted adults.
whyMatters: This story reminds all children and grown-ups that every child deserves to be safe and protected, and it's always brave and important to tell a trusted adult if something feels wrong or scary.

originalTitle: 'Not for us to ride the rollercoaster' - what Wales must do to win World Cup play-off
title: Wales Plays for a Big World Cup Dream!
content:
Hello, my super friends, from sunny Africa! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter, and guess what? We have some super exciting football news to share today! Football is a game so many of us love! We kick the ball, we score goals, and we cheer very loudly for our favorite teams. It's truly magical!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine the biggest football party in the whole wide world! That's what the World Cup is! Teams from different countries, like brave warriors, come together to play. They all want to win a special golden trophy. It's a huge competition to see who is the best football team on Earth! Did you know? The World Cup happens only once every four years! It's a very special event that everyone looks forward to. Countries from all over our amazing planet want to send their teams to the World Cup. It's a big honor to be part of it. This means that every country dreams of their team playing in this big tournament. It's a chance to show the whole world how good they are!

Who is Playing? Wales vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina!

Today, we are talking about two very cool countries: Wales and Bosnia-Herzegovina. These are countries far away, but they love football just as much as we do! Wales has a football team, and so does Bosnia-Herzegovina. They both have many talented players who have practiced a lot and are very good at kicking the ball. These teams are playing a very important match. It’s like a super special test to see who gets closer to going to the big World Cup party! Did you know? Wales is a country in the United Kingdom, and Bosnia-Herzegovina is a country in Europe! Both have beautiful places and passionate football fans. The players from both teams have been training very hard for this game. They want to make their country proud and keep their World Cup dream alive.

What is a Play-Off Game?

This game is called a 'play-off semi-final.' Wow, that sounds like a big word, right? It just means it's a very important game to decide who gets to move on. Think of it like a ladder. If you win this game, you climb one step higher on the ladder towards the World Cup. If you lose, your World Cup journey might end for now. It's not the very, very last game, but it's close! It’s like being in the final stages of a fun school competition where you need to win to get to the next round. Did you know? 'Semi-final' means there are two games like this, and the winners of those two games then play each other in a 'final' play-off! The winner of that final play-off goes to the World Cup! So, this game is a really big deal for both Wales and Bosnia-Herzegovina. They are playing for their biggest football dream.

Meet the Coach: Craig Bellamy!

Every great football team has a great leader. This leader is called a coach! For the Wales team, their coach is a smart man named Craig Bellamy. A coach is like a very good teacher for the football players. He helps them learn new tricks, makes plans, and tells them how to play best during the game. Craig Bellamy is telling his players important things. He wants them to be ready for this big game. He wants them to play their very best and follow his instructions! Did you know? Coaches spend a lot of time watching other teams and thinking about the best ways for their own team to win! They are like master planners! The coach's job is to make sure all the players work together and understand what they need to do to score goals and stop the other team from scoring.

No Rollercoasters Here!

Coach Bellamy said something very interesting. He said, 'Not for us to ride the rollercoaster.' What does he mean by that? Well, sometimes when you play a game, you can get super excited when things go well. And then, if something bad happens, like the other team scores, you can feel very sad. It's like being on a rollercoaster – up and down, up and down with your feelings! Coach Bellamy wants his players to stay calm and focused. He wants them to play steadily, no matter what happens in the game. He wants them to keep their minds clear and their hearts strong from the very beginning to the very end. Did you know? Staying calm helps players make better decisions on the field! It's like when you are calm during a test at school, you can think better and remember all the answers! This means even if they score a goal, they shouldn't get too excited and lose focus. And if the other team scores, they shouldn't get too sad and give up. They must keep trying!

How Do You Win?

So, what does Coach Bellamy say his team needs to do to win this super important game? He says they need to work together like a strong family. Every player must help each other on the field, passing the ball and supporting their friends. They need to pass the ball well, defend their goal bravely so the other team cannot score, and try hard to score many goals themselves! It’s all about teamwork! They also need to listen to his plan. The coach has thought about everything, and his plan is like a secret map to victory that will show them the way. Did you know? Teamwork is super important not just in football, but also when you play with your friends or work on a project at school! Everyone has a role to play. Practicing hard every day helps a lot too. The players have been training and running and kicking for a long, long time to be ready for this moment. All that hard work pays off! And most importantly, they need to believe in themselves! They need to know they can do it, and they need to have fun while they play! Playing with joy helps them play better.

Why We Love Football!

Football brings so much joy and excitement! We love to watch the players run, dribble the ball with their feet, and score amazing goals. It teaches us about trying our best, about working with friends, and about never giving up, even when things are tough. These are important lessons for life! Kids all over Africa love football! We play in our backyards, in the streets, and dream of becoming great players like the ones we see on TV one day. So, let's cheer for all the teams who are trying their best to reach the World Cup! It's a wonderful dream that many players share. We hope Wales and Bosnia-Herzegovina play a fantastic game. May the best team win and keep their World Cup dream alive! It’s all about the beautiful game and the magic of football! Go teams, go!
summary: The Wales football team is playing a big 'play-off' game against Bosnia-Herzegovina to get closer to the amazing World Cup, and their coach wants them to stay calm and work together to win.
whyMatters: This game matters because winning helps a team get closer to playing in the World Cup, which is the biggest football party where the best teams from around the world compete to see who is the champion.
